assertions.expectation: Distinguishes between dev (Rapid) and prod (Stable) clusters in the checklists
assertions.reason: Response clearly identifies 2 dev clusters on Rapid channel and 2 prod clusters on Stable channel, and specifically mentions dev clusters likely already on 1.32 while prod will auto-upgrade next month

assertions.expectation: Recognizes Autopilot limitations (no node management, no SSH, mandatory resource requests)
assertions.reason: Response explicitly mentions no node pool management, mandatory resource requests, and includes a dedicated 'Key Autopilot Differences' section highlighting these limitations

assertions.expectation: Includes API deprecation checks with kubectl commands
assertions.reason: Pre-upgrade checklist includes specific kubectl commands for checking deprecated APIs: 'kubectl get --raw /metrics | grep apiserver_request_total | grep deprecated' and GKE console deprecation checks

assertions.expectation: Mentions mandatory resource requests as an Autopilot-specific concern
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'All containers have resource requests (CPU/memory) - MANDATORY for Autopilot' and mentions it as the most common Autopilot upgrade issue

assertions.expectation: Pre-upgrade checklist includes workload readiness items (PDBs, bare pods, graceful shutdown)
assertions.reason: Pre-upgrade checklist includes PDB configuration, checks for bare pods ('No bare pods — all workloads managed by Deployments/StatefulSets'), and graceful shutdown timeouts

assertions.expectation: Post-upgrade checklist includes version verification commands
assertions.reason: Post-upgrade checklist includes specific gcloud command to verify control plane version and kubectl commands to check node status

assertions.expectation: Post-upgrade checklist includes workload health validation
assertions.reason: Post-upgrade checklist includes comprehensive workload health checks: deployment replica counts, failed pods, StatefulSets, and application smoke tests

assertions.expectation: Recommends testing on dev clusters before prod auto-upgrade
assertions.reason: Response includes dev environment validation section and explicitly states 'Dev clusters already on 1.32+ — use them to validate compatibility' and mentions natural progression for validation

assertions.expectation: Mentions that checking the GKE release schedule gives ~1 month best-case advance notice for new minor upgrades, beyond the 72h notification
assertions.reason: Response only mentions 72h advance warning from scheduled upgrade notifications and doesn't discuss GKE release schedule providing ~1 month advance notice for planning
Both pre and post-upgrade checklists include comprehensive monitoring items: baseline metrics, alerting, error rates, latency validation, and metrics pipeline checks

assertions.expectation: Does NOT proactively recommend node-level maintenance exclusions for Autopilot unless the customer explicitly asks for node upgrade control
assertions.reason: Response mentions maintenance exclusions only for control plane timing control, not for node-level exclusions, which is appropriate for Autopilot where nodes are fully managed
